description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a reddish-brown hue and a visible grip section near the base. The axe head is made of metal, displaying a broad, asymmetrical blade with a weathered, gray finish and a pronounced cutting edge. The blade is mounted perpendicularly to the handle, with a pointed metal cap at the top of the handle, possibly for decorative or functional reinforcement. The handle appears to be octagonal in cross-section, providing a secure grip, and the lower portion is wrapped or textured for added control. The blade has a single edge and a flat poll, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service areas.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade, featuring a pointed cap and textured grip.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the pointed cap and the narrow profile of the blade and handle.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the full length of the curved handle and the broad, single-edged blade.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals the blade's edge profile and the handle's ergonomic curvature.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, octagonal cross-section, reddish-brown, textured grip at base, ergonomic for hand use.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad metal blade with a flat poll, asymmetrical shape, weathered finish, no beard, pronounced cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible top cap reinforcement.
